message:
chore(beads): file ft-nyvyl (mux TLS slow-loris accept-loop wedge)

Pure insertion in .beads/issues.jsonl (1 line added, 0 removed).

New bead: `ft-nyvyl — [HIGH] mux TLS listener accept() has no
handshake timeout → slow-loris wedges per-domain accept loop`.
The mux's per-domain accept loop calls TLS `accept()` without any
read deadline on the handshake phase, so a peer that opens the TCP
connection but never progresses the TLS handshake (the classic
slow-loris shape applied to TLS rather than HTTP) can hold the
accept loop indefinitely. Because the loop is per-domain, a single
bad peer can starve all legitimate clients on that domain.

Filed HIGH because the attack is cheap (one TCP connection per
stalled slot, no bytes needed after the initial SYN) and the
impact is full accept-loop denial on the targeted domain. Fix
will need both a `set_read_timeout` on the TLS stream before the
handshake call and a tokio `timeout` wrapper on the overall
accept future, so both the blocking-read and cancellation
surfaces are covered.

files.patch:
@@ -272,6 +272,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-nrbrp","title":"[HIGH] runtime recv_event collapses Cancelled into channel-close and can drop queued capture events","description":"Review finding in crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime.rs:216-217 and 2956-2990. The local helper `recv_event(runtime_cx, rx)` is `rx.recv(runtime_cx).await.ok()`, so it converts every receive error into `None`. Under runtime_compat, `mpsc::Receiver::recv(cx)` returns `Err(RecvError::Cancelled)` for a pre-cancelled cx. The capture relay loop treats `None` as end-of-stream and immediately `break`s, then closes `capture_ring_tx`. Repro: create a capture-ingress mpsc with queued events, pre-cancel the loop cx before the next `recv_event`, keep `shutdown_flag == false`; `recv_event` returns `None`, the relay exits, and buffered capture events are abandoned even though the ingress channel was not closed. This conflates cancellation with sender disconnect and can turn task-graph cancellation ordering into silent ingest data loss. Expected: preserve the error distinction so cancellation does not masquerade as channel closure; either retry/drain under shutdown policy or propagate an explicit cancelled reason instead of closing the ring as if producers were gone.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T18:19:33.517921Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T18:33:29.495488Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T18:33:29.495226Z","close_reason":"Fixed recv_event cancellation semantics; Cancelled no longer masquerades as channel close","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ingest","review"]}
 {"id":"ft-nrvmu","title":"Fix GH #45 vendored feature forwarding regression in CLI crate","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-31T17:47:42.859719Z","created_by":"Codex","updated_at":"2026-03-31T18:18:44.582630Z","closed_at":"2026-03-31T18:18:44.582257Z","close_reason":"Already fixed in current checkout","external_ref":"gh-45","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cargo","feature-flags","github-issue","vendored"],"comments":[{"id":2654,"issue_id":"ft-nrvmu","author":"Codex","text":"Started implementation for GH #45. Scope: forward the CLI crate vendored feature into frankenterm-core/vendored so a default ft build compiles again. Validation in progress via rch exec -- cargo check -p frankenterm --bin ft.","created_at":"2026-03-31T17:56:08Z"},{"id":2658,"issue_id":"ft-nrvmu","author":"jemanuel","text":"Checked the current checkout against HEAD and the CLI feature wiring for GH #45 is already present here: `vendored = ["frankenterm", "frankenterm-core/vendored"]` in crates/frankenterm/Cargo.toml. No additional code change was needed in this session.","created_at":"2026-03-31T18:18:14Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-ntvzq","title":"error_renderer JSON category should follow snake_case contract","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:41:41.387206Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:47:33.859542Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T09:47:33.859015Z","close_reason":"Already landed in 1bb10288 and 70f366af; current error_renderer.rs also has concurrent uncommitted edits, so I stood down rather than mix ownership.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"ft-nyvyl","title":"[HIGH] mux TLS listener accept() has no handshake timeout → slow-loris wedges per-domain accept loop","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T18:39:36.728112Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T18:40:04.003226Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review","runtime","security"],"comments":[{"id":3640,"issue_id":"ft-nyvyl","author":"jemanuel","text":"# Gap

crates/frankenterm-mux-server/src/ossl.rs:80-109:

    match stream {
        Ok(stream) => {
            stream.set_nodelay(true).ok();
            let acceptor = self.acceptor.clone();
            let dispatch_config = self.dispatch_config;
            match acceptor.accept(stream) {
                Ok(stream) => { ... spawn async dispatch ... }
                Err(e) => log::error!("failed TlsAcceptor: {}", e),
            }
        }
        ...
    }

Two problems compound:

1. `stream` is a `std::net::TcpStream` and nothing calls
   `set_read_timeout()` / `set_write_timeout()` on it before
   `acceptor.accept(stream)`. The OpenSSL handshake reads/writes on the
   raw blocking socket.
2. `acceptor.accept(stream)` is executed SYNCHRONOUSLY on the accept
   thread, not the async runtime. A peer that opens a TCP socket and
   refuses to advance the TLS handshake (or drip-feeds it a byte at a
   time) parks this thread indefinitely.

Because the accept loop iterates after accept() returns, a single slow /
malicious peer halts every subsequent legitimate connection for this
TLS domain.

# Comparison with client side

frankenterm/client/src/client.rs:1266-1267 does call `set_read_timeout`
and `set_write_timeout` on the client-side TCP stream before initiating
the handshake — so the operator-side connection is protected, but the
server-side isn't.

# Why this matters

This is a zero-credential DoS against the mux-server TLS domain. Any
peer that can reach the TLS port (even with an invalid cert — note
that peer-cert verification only runs AFTER accept() at line 88) can
wedge the listener.

The `set_read_timeout` landing was partial: ft-kovt3 wired it for the
client; this bead extends it to the mux-server accept path, which is
the remaining half.

# Shape of fix (≤30 LOC)

Before `acceptor.accept(stream)` in ossl.rs, set a reasonable handshake
deadline on the raw TCP stream, then clear it once the handshake
completes and before wrapping in AsyncSslStream:

    stream.set_read_timeout(Some(HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT))?;
    stream.set_write_timeout(Some(HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT))?;
    let stream = match acceptor.accept(stream) {
        Ok(s) => s,
        Err(e) => { log::error!("failed TlsAcceptor: {}", e); continue; }
    };
    stream.get_ref().set_read_timeout(None).ok();
    stream.get_ref().set_write_timeout(None).ok();

A constant like `const HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT: Duration = Duration::from_secs(10);`
is conservative and aligned with OpenSSL session ticket recommendations.

The `continue` (rather than `break`) on accept failure is also worth a
second look while the file is open — the current `break` at line 90 for
verify_peer_cert errors exits the whole accept loop, which is a
separate DoS lever.

# Out of scope

The select-arm-drop / registration concern raised in the audit is
working as designed (the stream outlives the dropped future; the
IoRegistration lives on the stream, not on the dropped future). No bead.

The capture→relay→persistence shutdown ordering in runtime.rs is also
correct: capture.await completes before relay.await, and relay closes
the ring BEFORE persistence.await is entered (runtime.rs:4124-4141).
No bead.

The prune_segments_sync / append_segment_sync race is bounded by
SQLite's single-writer rule on the DB file; the fts_pane_progress
rewind runs inside the same implicit transaction as the DELETE
(storage.rs:14157-14196). No bead.

The ft-nrbrp Cancelled-collapse pattern is already handled in the
relay loop via explicit RecvEvent::{Item, Closed, Cancelled} match
(runtime.rs:2986-3010). No follow-up bead.","created_at":"2026-04-24T18:40:04Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-o4xi3","title":"Extend proptest_mcp + proptest_mcp_bridge structural invariants","description":"proptest_mcp.rs (3.7KB) and proptest_mcp_bridge.rs (4.0KB) are thin. Add structural protocol invariants: all tool names follow 'wa.' or 'frankenterm.' convention, all resource URIs use a wa:// scheme, all resource templates contain {} placeholders, tool/resource/template sets are stable across rebuilds, initialize response is deterministic for the same config, and the client-side tool list matches the server for multiple suffixes simultaneously. Complements the ft-mejto golden without duplicating it.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T05:38:12.628263Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T06:06:42.089459Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T06:06:42.089037Z","close_reason":"proptest_mcp_bridge extended with 5 new structural invariants (wa.* namespace, wa:// scheme, resource/template disjointness, build idempotency, non-empty descriptions). proptest_mcp.rs left untouched due to pre-existing test-binary flakiness with >3 proptests (server threads leak).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mcp","proptest","testing"]}
 {"id":"ft-o657v","title":"conformance_mcp_manifest: pin tool input_schema additionalProperties:false invariant","description":"# Gap

`crates/frankenterm-core/tests/conformance_mcp_manifest.rs` rejects unknown top-level fields (via `#[serde(deny_unknown_fields)]`), checks uniqueness of tool/resource/prompt names, and validates minimal/optional field presence. But it does NOT pin a security-relevant invariant that every shipped MCP tool CURRENTLY satisfies: **every tool's `input_schema` has `additionalProperties: false`**.

Verified by:

    $ rg -c 'additionalProperties' crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs
    29
    $ rg -c 'input_schema:' crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs
    29

All 29 input_schemas currently set it. Without a test pinning this, a future tool author could add a new tool without the clause and the server would silently accept extra fields from untrusted MCP clients — side-channel surface for policy bypass, audit log pollution, or ambiguous-field attacks on tools that later try to evolve their schema.

# Fix

Add `every_tool_input_schema_denies_additional_properties` in conformance_mcp_manifest.rs that iterates `server.tools()` from `build_server_with_db(&Config::default(), Some(...))` and asserts each `tool.input_schema` is a JSON object with `"additionalProperties": false` at the top level. If a tool's schema is NOT type=object (e.g. `{}`, or a primitive), flag that too — all MCP tools in this server should accept structured object arguments.

# Non-fix

Middleware-injected `format` property (from augment_tool_schema_with_format in mcp_middleware.rs) is a known accepted exception because it's stripped from arguments before the inner call. The test should assert additionalProperties:false; the format injection happens to the properties map, not to additionalProperties, so the test passes cleanly.

# Severity

MEDIUM — no live defect; every current tool sets the bit. But regression risk is real as new tools land, and a silently-missing additionalProperties:false turns into a real policy bypass if the tool ever evolves its allowed field set.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:03:57.448008Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T12:06:52.683351Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T12:06:52.683105Z","close_reason":"Landed as part of f73e139a (absorbed commit). every_tool_input_schema_denies_additional_properties iterates server.tools() from build_server_with_db + asserts each input_schema is type=object with additionalProperties literal false — pins the 29/29 invariant so future tools without the clause break CI instead of silently widening the accepted field set.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-odywh","title":"mux local spawn offload regression coverage","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-22T04:24:40.694101Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T04:30:56.950244Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T04:30:56.949848Z","close_reason":"Verified in f8e260d7","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
